  "title": "ESG, QHSE & Compliance Manager",
  "description": "KannyVic Enterprise Consult - a fast-growing agribusiness and climate-smart infrastructure company is seeking a passionate and highly organized candidates to support the planning, delivery, and growth of our agricultural training and capacity-building programs in the capacity below:

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: ESG, QHSE &amp; Compliance Manager

Job Location: Warri, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	We are looking for an experienced ESG, QHSE &amp; Compliance Manager to lead our Environmental, Social, Governance (ESG) agenda, Health &amp; Safety systems, and compliance framework across operations.
	The role ensures that all projects and activities meet required regulatory, environmental, safety, quality, and sustainability standards while supporting continuous improvement across the business.
	This position plays a critical leadership role in safeguarding people, protecting the environment, maintaining quality standards, and ensuring compliance with both local regulations and donor/investor requirements.


Key Responsibilities
ESG &amp; Sustainability:


	Drive ESG strategy alignment across all operations and projects
	Track, measure, and report ESG and SDG performance indicators
	Lead sustainability and climate-smart reporting initiatives
	Support donor and investor ESG compliance reporting


Health, Safety &amp; Environment (HSE):


	Develop and enforce health and safety policies and procedures
	Conduct risk assessments across sites and operations
	Ensure proper PPE usage and worker safety compliance
	Lead incident reporting, investigation, and corrective actions
	Oversee environmental compliance (waste, water, effluent, chemicals, emissions)


Quality, Compliance &amp; Systems:


	Establish and maintain QHSE management systems
	Coordinate ISO readiness and certification processes (ISO 9001, 14001, 45001 basics)
	Ensure adherence to quality control standards across operations
	Maintain regulatory compliance documentation and audit readiness


Operations Oversight:


	Monitor contractor and site compliance with safety and quality standards
	Review operational practices to ensure alignment with company policies
	Identify gaps and implement corrective and preventive measures


Authority &amp; Decision Rights:
This role carriesstop-work authorityand may:


	Stop any unsafe work immediately
	Reject work that does not meet required quality standards
	Escalate serious compliance, safety, or environmental issues directly to the CEO/MD
	Recommend suspension of non-compliant operations or contractors where necessary


Requirements
Experience:


	5&ndash;10 years of experience in ESG, HSE, compliance, environmental management, agriculture, or construction environments
	Proven experience managing safety systems and compliance frameworks


Technical Skills:


	Working knowledge of ISO standards (9001, 14001, 45001 or equivalents)
	Experience in HSE reporting and incident management systems
	Strong understanding of ESG/SDG frameworks and sustainability reporting
	Risk assessment and mitigation planning experience
	Familiarity with regulatory compliance in operational/project environments


Soft Skills:


	Strong leadership and authority in compliance enforcement
	Excellent reporting and documentation skills
	Attention to detail and high level of accountability
	Ability to influence teams and enforce standards across departments


What We Offer


	Opportunity to shape ESG and compliance systems at organizational level
	Leadership role with direct access to executive management
	Exposure to donor-funded, infrastructure, and sustainability-driven projects
	Competitive remuneration aligned with experience

ESG, QHSE & Compliance Manager Job at KannyVic Enterprise Consult




        
    

        
        KannyVic Enterprise Consult - a fast-growing agribusiness and climate-smart infrastructure company is seeking a passionate and highly organized candidates to support the planning, delivery, and growth of our agricultural training and capacity-building programs in the capacity below:

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: ESG, QHSE & Compliance Manager

Job Location: Warri, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for an experienced ESG, QHSE & Compliance Manager to lead our Environmental, Social, Governance (ESG) agenda, Health & Safety systems, and compliance framework across operations.
	The role ensures that all projects and activities meet required regulatory, environmental, safety, quality, and sustainability standards while supporting continuous improvement across the business.
	This position plays a critical leadership role in safeguarding people, protecting the environment, maintaining quality standards, and ensuring compliance with both local regulations and donor/investor requirements.


Key Responsibilities
ESG & Sustainability:


	Drive ESG strategy alignment across all operations and projects
	Track, measure, and report ESG and SDG performance indicators
	Lead sustainability and climate-smart reporting initiatives
	Support donor and investor ESG compliance reporting


Health, Safety & Environment (HSE):


	Develop and enforce health and safety policies and procedures
	Conduct risk assessments across sites and operations
	Ensure proper PPE usage and worker safety compliance
	Lead incident reporting, investigation, and corrective actions
	Oversee environmental compliance (waste, water, effluent, chemicals, emissions)


Quality, Compliance & Systems:


	Establish and maintain QHSE management systems
	Coordinate ISO readiness and certification processes (ISO 9001, 14001, 45001 basics)
	Ensure adherence to quality control standards across operations
	Maintain regulatory compliance documentation and audit readiness


Operations Oversight:


	Monitor contractor and site compliance with safety and quality standards
	Review operational practices to ensure alignment with company policies
	Identify gaps and implement corrective and preventive measures


Authority & Decision Rights:
This role carriesstop-work authorityand may:


	Stop any unsafe work immediately
	Reject work that does not meet required quality standards
	Escalate serious compliance, safety, or environmental issues directly to the CEO/MD
	Recommend suspension of non-compliant operations or contractors where necessary


Requirements
Experience:


	5–10 years of experience in ESG, HSE, compliance, environmental management, agriculture, or construction environments
	Proven experience managing safety systems and compliance frameworks


Technical Skills:


	Working knowledge of ISO standards (9001, 14001, 45001 or equivalents)
	Experience in HSE reporting and incident management systems
	Strong understanding of ESG/SDG frameworks and sustainability reporting
	Risk assessment and mitigation planning experience
	Familiarity with regulatory compliance in operational/project environments


Soft Skills:


	Strong leadership and authority in compliance enforcement
	Excellent reporting and documentation skills
	Attention to detail and high level of accountability
	Ability to influence teams and enforce standards across departments


What We Offer


	Opportunity to shape ESG and compliance systems at organizational level
	Leadership role with direct access to executive management
	Exposure to donor-funded, infrastructure, and sustainability-driven projects
	Competitive remuneration aligned with experience


Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Finance Manager Job at LAGRIDE Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        LAGRIDE Nigeria Limited, a subsidiary of CIG Motor Nigeria Limited, is a forward-thinking e-hailing company revolutionizing urban mobility through innovative and sustainable transportation solutions. As a proudly indigenous brand, we are committed to delivering safe, efficient, and affordable ride services powered by cutting-edge technology and electric vehicles. With a strong focus on customer satisfaction, reliability, and innovation, LAGRIDE is redefining how people move across Lagos and setting new standards for the future of mobility in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment type: Full-time

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Finance Manager is responsible for overseeing the financial health and strategic financial management of LAGRIDE.
	The role focuses on financial planning, budgeting, reporting, cost control, regulatory compliance, cash flow management, and financial risk oversight.
	The Finance Manager plays a critical role in supporting executive decision-making, ensuring operational efficiency, and driving profitability across fleet and corporate operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and manage annual budgets, forecasts, and financial plans aligned with business objectives.
	Prepare and present monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports to management.
	Monitor daily rental revenue, driver payments, and fleet financial performance.
	Conduct variance analysis and provide strategic insights to improve profitability.
	Manage cash flow planning, treasury operations, and banking relationships.
	Ensure compliance with Nigerian tax laws and statutory obligations (VAT, PAYE, WHT, etc.).
	Coordinate internal and external audits and liaise with tax consultants and regulators.
	Oversee accounts payable, receivable, reconciliations, and financial controls.
	Implement and strengthen internal control systems to prevent fraud and financial leakages.
	Monitor operational costs and recommend cost optimization strategies.
	Support fleet expansion planning, capital investments, and funding strategies.
	Supervise and mentor finance team members to ensure timely and accurate reporting.
	Develop and update financial policies and procedures in line with best practices.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related field.
	Professional certification (ICAN, ACCA, or equivalent) is required.
	5–8 years of progressive finance experience, with at least 2 years in a managerial or supervisory role.
	Strong knowledge of financial reporting standards and Nigerian tax regulations.
	Experience in mobility, logistics, leasing, or service-based industries is an advantage.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, accounting software, and financial reporting tools.
	Strong leadership and stakeholder management skills.


Remuneration


	Competitive salary and performance-based incentives.
	Health and wellness benefits (HMO).
	Career growth and professional development opportunities.
	Opportunity to play a key role in strengthening Lagride’s financial sustainability and expansion strategy.

  "title": "Executive Assistant",
  "description": "HOP Services has rapidly expanded to meet the demands of the fast-growing Hospitality industry. Our strong management team, comprising experienced professionals with technical expertise, drives our growth. At HOP Services, we believe that service delivery is essential for quality guest experience and satisfaction. We strive to deliver exceptional services that exceed our clients expectations. Our satisfied clients are a testament to our success.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Assistant
Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Overview


	We are seeking a highly organised, proactive, and tech-savvy Executive Assistant to support executive operations, scheduling, planning, and administrative coordination within a fast-paced work environment.
	The ideal candidate must be intentional, detail-oriented, educated, and able to manage multiple responsibilities efficiently while staying up to date with modern digital tools and workplace systems.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage executive calendars, appointments, meetings, and reminders.
	Coordinate schedules, follow-ups, and daily operational activities.
	Assist with planning, budgeting, reporting, and administrative tasks.
	Organise digital documents, records, and files using Google Drive and related tools.
	Prepare meeting notes, task updates, and operational summaries when required.
	Support project coordination and ensure timely task execution.
	Maintain effective communication and workflow across teams.
	Use AI tools and digital productivity systems to improve efficiency.
	Monitor timelines and deadlines, and ensure smooth daily operations.
	And other duties as assigned.


Qualifications


	Must be a university graduate.
	Strong computer and administrative skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and Google Workspace.
	Strong knowledge of Google Drive, calendar management, and scheduling tools.
	AI-savvy and familiar with modern productivity tools.
	Excellent organisational and time management abilities.
	Strong written and verbal communication skills.
	Ability to multitask and work independently in a fast-paced environment.
	Professional, focused, and detail-oriented.
	Ability to think proactively and solve problems efficiently.


Preferred Skills:


	Experience with budget planning and operational coordination.
	Familiarity with Notion, Trello, Slack, or related productivity tools is a plus.
	Strong documentation and reporting skills.
	Ability to maintain confidentiality and professionalism at all times.


What You&rsquo;ll Gain


	Salary: N100,000 - N120,000 per month.
	Hands-on experience working closely with executive management.
	Exposure to operations, planning, and strategic coordination.
	Opportunity for professional growth and long-term career development.
	Potential for increased responsibilities and leadership opportunities based on performance.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should their CV and Cover Letter to: vera.jonah@hopcleaning.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

	The Logistics Associate is responsible for supporting the efficient execution of logistics, order fulfillment, and delivery operations across Ashcorp&rsquo;s brands.

	Coordinate customer feedback systems to gather insights for service improvement and innovation.

	Coordinate with inventory and operations teams to ensure alignment between stock availability and order fulfillment activities.
	Support quality control processes to ensure products are delivered accurately and in excellent condition.
	Assist in maintaining operational records and logistics documentation.


Compliance &amp; Risk Management:


	Monitor compliance with logistics regulations, transportation standards, and company policies.
	Maintain up-to-date records of asset renewal documentation and other logistics-related compliance requirements.
	Support risk identification and escalation processes to minimize disruptions in the supply chain.


Process Improvement &amp; Reporting:


	Continuously identify opportunities to improve logistics processes, reduce costs, and enhance operational efficiency.
	Contribute innovative ideas and initiatives that support business growth and team collaboration.
	Generate logistics reports and provide insights on delivery performance, fulfillment accuracy, customer satisfaction, and operational costs.

	On-time Delivery Rate: Measure the percentage of orders that are delivered to customers within the stipulated time frame.
	Client relationship management: Building and maintaining strong relationships with clients to ensure customer satisfaction and retention.

	Cost per order: Calculate the total cost incurred to fulfill each customer order including transportation, and other incurred costs.

	Strong understanding of logistics operations, transportation processes, and supply chain management principles.
	Experience managing courier relationships and coordinating delivery operations.
	Proficiency in Order Management Systems (OMS), ERP systems, and Microsoft Office applications.
	Strong communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.
	Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities.
	High attention to detail and commitment to operational accuracy.
	Strong time management and organizational skills.
	Customer-focused mindset with the ability to deliver exceptional service experiences.
	Ability to adapt to changing priorities and work effectively in a fast-paced environment.

	Commitment to ethical conduct, integrity, and professionalism in all business activities.
